svg.text: How the parse example AST is allocated in an oxc-style bump allocator

g.text: Source Text (heap-allocated, owned by caller — separate from arena)
"/** Find a user.\n * See {@link UserService} for details.\n * @param {string|number} id - The user ID ... */"

svg.text: Sequential allocations — bump_ptr advances on each alloc()

g.text: ↑ ptr points into SOURCE TEXT, not into arena (zero-copy)
TypeExpr<'a> (16 bytes — uniform, all variants boxed)
Each alloc() advances the bump pointer. Nodes are packed contiguously.
Total: ~2 KB for this example (32 allocations × ~64 bytes average).
Text.value and TagName.value are &'a str slices into the source text.
No string data is copied into the arena.
Raw pointers into the arena. No Drop, no reference counting.
Option<Box> uses niche: None = null pointer (still 8 bytes).
4. Vec<'a, T> backing storage in arena
Data pointer → contiguous array within the same arena.
Pre-allocated with estimated capacity to avoid reallocation.
5. O(1) bulk deallocation — no individual destructors called
Source Text (heap) "/** Find a user.\n * See {@link UserService} for details.\n * @param {string|number} id - The user ID ... */"
Green text inside Text/TagName nodes = &'a str zero-copy reference into Source Text above (no heap copy)
Red solid arrows: Box<'a, T> — pointers between arena-allocated nodes
Blue solid arrows: Vec<'a, T> — data pointers to arena-allocated contiguous arrays
Green dashed arrows: &'a str — zero-copy references to the source text (the only pointers escaping the arena)
allocator.reset() → ALL nodes freed in O(1)
bump_ptr rewinds to start. Chunk retained for next parse (cache-warm reuse).
